item.title: Wales loss fuels Flying Fijians ahead of England test
item.description: Following last week's disappointing defeat to Wales, the loss has added fuel to the fire as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare for a crucial clash against England tomorrow morning. Speaking from England, Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere says the team is ready for the challenge after a strong week of preparation.
item.content:encoded:
Following last week's disappointing defeat to Wales, the loss has added fuel to the fire as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare for a crucial clash against England tomorrow morning.

Speaking from England, Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere says the team is ready for the challenge after a strong week of preparation.

Ikanivere says the squad has put in the hard work throughout the week and is now focused on delivering a much-improved performance against England.

He adds that the players are eager to showcase their preparation when they take the field tomorrow.

The Flying Fijians will take on England at 1.10am tomorrow at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

Meanwhile in today's matches, New Zealand will face Italy at 5.05pm at Sky Stadium, Australia will host France at 7.40pm in Brisbane, and Ireland will take on Japan at 10.10pm.

Tomorrow, South Africa will play Scotland at 3.10am in Pretoria, while Wales will face Argentina at 7.10am in San Juan.

item.title: Merino's late winner sends Spain into World Cup semi finals
item.description: Merino's late winner sends Spain into World Cup semi finals

item.title: Seruvakula backs Flying Fijians to deliver against England
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026 FIJI Water Flying Fijians coach Senirusi Seruvakula says he believes the players selected can deliver a strong performance when they take on England in the Nations Championship this Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026

FIJI Water Flying Fijians coach Senirusi Seruvakula says he believes the players selected can deliver a strong performance when they take on England in the Nations Championship this Sunday.

Speaking to the media from England, Seruvakula says the team learned valuable lessons from last week's match against Wales and worked on those areas during preparations this week.

Seruvakula has made several changes to the starting XV that lost to Wales, with Levani Botia named at number eight, Peceli Yato at blindside flanker, Caleb Muntz at fly-half, Simione Kuruvoli at halfback and Vuate Karawalevu on the right wing.

The Flying Fijians will take on England at 1.10am on Sunday at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

In other international fixtures this weekend, New Zealand will face Italy at 5.05pm tomorrow at Sky Stadium, Australia will host France at 7.40pm tomorrow in Brisbane, and Ireland will take on Japan at 10.10pm tomorrow.

On Sunday, South Africa will play Scotland at 3.10am in Pretoria, while Wales will face Argentina at 7.10am in San Juan.

item.title: Khan-Pereira stars in Warriors 32-6 victory over Tigers
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 11/07/2026 24-year-old Alofiana Khan-Pereira scored a double as the Warriors thumped the Wests Tigers 32-6 in the NRL last night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 11/07/2026

24-year-old Alofiana Khan-Pereira scored a double as the Warriors thumped the Wests Tigers 32-6 in the NRL last night.

The visitors raced to a 14-0 lead within the opening 18 minutes, thanks to standout performances from James Fisher-Harris, Erin Clark and Tanner Stowers-Smith. The Warriors were also helped by Bunty Afoa, who spent 10 minutes in the sin bin for a late tackle against his former club.

The Warriors crossed for six tries, while the Tigers managed just one.

The win keeps the Warriors in second place on the NRL table with 28 points.

In today's matches, the Dolphins take on the Sharks at 5pm, the Bulldogs face the Raiders at 7.30pm, and the Roosters meet the Eels at 9.35pm.

item.title: Fiji Pearls defeat Papua New Guinea 64-35
item.description: The Fiji Pearls secured their first victory of the Spirit Series in Sydney, Australia, with a commanding 64-35 win over Papua New Guinea yesterday in their third match. The players showed huge improvement from their last two games after falling short against Singapore during their first game and against the Australian Black Swan in their second game.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Pearls secured their first victory of the Spirit Series in Sydney, Australia, with a commanding 64-35 win over Papua New Guinea yesterday in their third match.

The players showed huge improvement from their last two games after falling short against Singapore during their first game and against the Australian Black Swan in their second game.

Fijian supporters packed the game's venue, cheering the players on to their first victory, which boosted the morale of the players.

Fiji now prepares to face PNG again for the bronze medal in the four-nation championship.

item.title: Ratu Latianara, Dudley and RSM College set for Marist clash in Southern Zone quarter finals
item.description: Ratu Latianara College, Dudley High School, and Ratu Simione Matanitobua College are gearing up for a crucial Vodafone Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition matches against Marist Brothers High School tomorrow. Ratu Latianara will take on Marist in the Under-17 grade, while Dudley High School faces Marist in the Under-16 divison.
item.content:encoded:
Ratu Latianara College, Dudley High School, and Ratu Simione Matanitobua College are gearing up for a crucial Vodafone Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition matches against Marist Brothers High School tomorrow.

Ratu Latianara will take on Marist in the Under-17 grade, while Dudley High School faces Marist in the Under-16 divison.

Ratu Simione Matanitobua College will meet Marist in the Under-15 category.

When contacted by fijivillage News, officials from the Kaunikuila brigade say they would not comment on the teams' preparations, confirming they remain on a media blackout.

The three-time Deans finalists are keeping their cards close to their chest this year, choosing to let their performances on the field do the talking.

The Southern Zone quarter-finals will kick off tomorrow at the Suva Grammar School Grounds and the Veiuto Grounds.

In other matches, Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School will take on Dudley High School in the Under-14 grade, while MGM faces Nabua Secondary School in the Under-16 division.

In the Under-17 grade, Naitasiri Secondary School meets Lomary Secondary School.

There are no Under-18 Southern Zone matches scheduled this week, as the top teams have automatically qualified for next week's zone semi-finals.

item.title: We need to be accurate about the little things - Seruvakula
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026 With just a few days left before the FIJI Water Flying Fijians take on England in their second Nations Championship match, coach Senirusi Seruvakula says they will need to be accurate in the little things they do.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026

With just a few days left before the FIJI Water Flying Fijians take on England in their second Nations Championship match, coach Senirusi Seruvakula says they will need to be accurate in the little things they do.

Reflecting on last week's loss to Wales, Seruvakula stressed that the team needs to improve in key areas such as passing, offloads, scrum timing, movement and defence.

He says they will also need to be patient when they have the ball in hand.

He adds that everyone is fit and ready for this week's match.

The Flying Fijians will take on England at 1.10am on Sunday at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

In other international fixtures this weekend, New Zealand will play Italy at 5.05pm tommorow at Sky Stadium, Australia will host France at 7.40pm on Saturday in Brisbane, and Ireland will take on Japan at 10.10pm on tomorrow.

On Sunday, South Africa will face Scotland at 3.10am in Pretoria, while Wales will meet Argentina at 7.10am in San Juan.

item.title: England's Jarell Quansah suspended for two World Cup matches after Mexico red card
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026 England will be without centre-back Jarell Quansah for Sunday's quarter-final clash with Norway after he received a two-match suspension following his red card in the Round of 16 victory over Mexico.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 10/07/2026

England will be without centre-back Jarell Quansah for Sunday's quarter-final clash with Norway after he received a two-match suspension following his red card in the Round of 16 victory over Mexico.

Quansah was sent off in the 54th minute of England's 3-2 win after a VAR review upgraded his challenge on Mexico defender Jesús Gallardo to a red card for a high tackle.

England played the final 36 minutes with 10 men but held on to record a historic victory.

Quansah will miss the quarter-final against Norway and, should England progress, the semi-final as well.

The suspension leaves England short of defensive options on the right side.

They are also sweating on the fitness of Reece James, who has been sidelined with a hamstring injury since the goalless draw with Ghana.

James trained separately from the main group on Wednesday as he continued to follow an individual programme.

England hopes James will be fit to face Norway. Otherwise, Thomas Tuchel will have more selection headaches.

In the other quarter-finals, Spain will face Belgium at 7am tomorrow, while Argentina meets Switzerland at 1pm Sunday.

Meanwhile, France is the first team through to the semi finals after beating Morocco 2-0.

item.title: France beat Morocco 2-0 to reach 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals
item.description: France are the first team through to the semi-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after beating Morocco 2-0 in the quarter-finals.Bo

item.title: Vunimono High and Nakasi High to meet in Rewa Secondary Schools U15 and U17 finals
item.description: Vunimono High School has defeated Saraswati College 2-0 to book their place in the final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football As
